Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows evidence of significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), mild hypoglycemia, and abnormal liver enzyme (alkaline phosphatase). Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, and proteinuria without signs of infection. These findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another chronic kidney disease, with possible hepatic involvement. Clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.
